incident_summary.root_cause: Deployment lost on Vercel (DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND) — likely infrastructure/build issue
incident_summary.resolution_method: Git push to main → Vercel auto-redeploy → successful recovery
incident_summary.detection_gap: CTB reported false positives at 05:43, 05:55, 06:01 KST — actual status not validated

post_recovery_metrics.deployment_stability: 170+ hours continuous uptime (incident was brief interruption)

incident_timeline.detail: Vercel returned HTTP 404 for all routes (126+ min duration initial estimate)
CTB reported HTTP 200 stable (8h+) without validation
Actual Vercel check returned DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND error
Pushed main branch to trigger Vercel redeploy
All endpoints validated — deployment fully operational

recommended_actions.reason: 3 consecutive false positives (05:43, 05:55, 06:01) while incident was ongoing
Git push trigger for Vercel redeploy worked — confirm this as standard recovery procedure

recommended_actions.action: Verify no data loss or inconsistency from incident
recommended_actions.reason: 59-minute deployment outage may affect data synchronization

lessons_learned.monitoring_gap: CTB system can report false positives when monitoring endpoints without proper error handling validation
lessons_learned.recovery_success: Git push to main triggered automatic Vercel redeploy — effective recovery method
lessons_learned.uptime_resilience: Despite 59-minute outage, no user-facing data loss; all 4 P1 projects remained code-complete
